summary: Retest after fix verified. OrderStatusPatch model now declares `cancel_reason: Optional[str] = None` at server.py L205. Reran /app/backend/tests/test_iteration4_cancel_reason.py — 12/12 tests PASS (previously 10/12). PATCH /api/orders/{id}/status with status='cancelled' + cancel_reason now returns 200 and persists both cancel_reason and cancelled_at. Normal lifecycle (received → preparing → out_for_delivery → delivered) and cancel-without-reason path also pass. All regression endpoints (admin login, GET /products=38, GET /promotions=3, POST /delivery/quote, POST /orders, GET /orders/mine, GET /auth/me, GET /settings, GET /cep/{cep}) work as expected. Tests run against http://localhost:8001 per main agent instruction (preview URL Cloudflare/ingress 404 still pending platform fix; not in scope).

minor.issue: Handler only persists cancel_reason when both status=='cancelled' AND cancel_reason is truthy. Empty-string cancel_reason is silently dropped (no 422). Acceptable per spec but consider adding `min_length=1` and/or `max_length` to the Pydantic field for stricter input validation.

action_items: (Out of scope here) Platform team to fix Cloudflare/ingress 404 on https://gratinalli-spets.preview.emergentagent.com/api/* so external clients reach the (already-correct) backend.
critical_code_review_comments: Fix is minimal and correct: one-line addition of cancel_reason field on OrderStatusPatch matches the handler at L535-550.
Still no state-transition validation in update_order_status — e.g., a 'delivered' order can be moved back to 'received'. Not blocking, but worth a future enhancement.
server.py is 914 lines — still exceeds the 700-line guideline. Consider splitting into routers (auth, orders, products, settings, delivery) when next touched.
seed_data_creation: Tests create TEST_it4_* customer + several orders against localhost. No teardown needed (prefixed test data).
context_for_next_testing_agent: iteration_5: cancel_reason bug is FIXED. 12/12 backend tests pass against localhost:8001. Public preview URL still returns Cloudflare 404 on /api/* — that is a platform/ingress issue, not a code bug. test_iteration4_cancel_reason.py auto-falls back to localhost so it stays usable for regression once the public URL is restored. No further backend retesting needed unless a new feature is shipped.
rca of the issue: Previously: OrderStatusPatch (Pydantic v2) did not declare cancel_reason, so Pydantic stripped the field from the request and the handler crashed with AttributeError when reading body.cancel_reason. Fix applied at /app/backend/server.py L205: `cancel_reason: Optional[str] = None`. Verified by pytest (test_patch_cancelled_persists_reason + test_patch_cancelled_no_reason + test_preparing_with_cancel_reason_does_not_crash all PASS) — order now returns with cancel_reason and cancelled_at populated when status='cancelled' and reason is provided; cancel_reason is correctly ignored for non-cancelled statuses.
